trans_text:
137
CUNEIFORM TABLETS OF THE SCHONEVELD COLLECTION
D. NEO-BABYLONIAN TEXTS (nOS. 18-20)
by A.C.V.M. BONGENAAR1)
"Letter of Itti-Nabû-baltu to
Niqudu, my father.
May Nabû and Marduk ordain the
health and life of my father!
The unripe dates of Nabú-mukin-apli
about which you said to Balassu
as follows: 'Send a messenger
with Ina-t-tir'-
When a messenger came
along with him, he
has built an enclosed area (to store dates).
The gardeners did not agree
(saying) as follows: 'Give us (our)
promissory notes (first)'.
They have (already) beaten up
Nabû-kusuranni,
and his garment
they have taken away.
When it will be convenient,
come one day to me, because
until we go (there), they (=the gardeners)
will be angry. The dates
they will cut off and
you will not achieve your objective.
Whoever will say to you
about Ina-ti-tir as follows:
'Ina-ti-tir is lazy' (and you will believe it),
(and) when (as a consequence) you will
take away his share(?),
may Nabû (then) finish (this matter)
